messages.content:
What is one of the reasons to consider implementing a blockchain solution?


inability to trace and access data of other users


ability to verify that data has not been tampered with since its creation


inability to track simultaneous updates by multiple users


ability to work on different versions of the same data


I don't know this yet.
One of the reasons to consider implementing a blockchain solution is the ability to verify that data has not been tampered with since its creation.

Accenture has put together a coalition of several ecosystem partners to implement the principles of blockchain and Multi-party Systems using varying technologies. One group of partners is referred to as MAAG.

Which companies are included in the MAAG group?


Morgan Stanley, Apple, Anheuser Busch, General Motors


Mitsubishi, Agricultural Bank of China, Apple, Goldman Sachs


MetLife, American Express, Alphabet Inc., General Electric


Microsoft, Amazon, Alibaba, Google


I don't know this yet.

I'm sorry, I don't have access to current information or the ability to browse the internet. Therefore, I can't provide you with the specific companies included in the MAAG group. It's always best to refer to reliable sources or the official announcements from Accenture to get the most up-to-date and accurate information.

Accenture has put together a coalition of several ecosystem partners to implement the principles of blockchain and Multi-party Systems using varying technologies. One group of partners is referred to as MAAG.

Which companies are included in the MAAG group?


Morgan Stanley, Apple, Anheuser Busch, General Motors


Mitsubishi, Agricultural Bank of China, Apple, Goldman Sachs


MetLife, American Express, Alphabet Inc., General Electric


Microsoft, Amazon, Alibaba, Google


I don't know this yet.

I'm sorry, but I don't have access to current information about specific coalitions or partnerships formed by Accenture. It's always best to refer to reliable sources or the official announcements from Accenture to get the most accurate and up-to-date information about their partnerships.

What type of transaction cannot be stored in blocks on a blockchain?


all types of transactions can be stored in blocks on a blockchain


a transaction that issues token for a carton of milk from Beach Farm in Wisconsin


a transaction that assigns copyright ownership to a song


a transaction that issues a degree to Mary Wilson from Purdue University in Electrical Engineering


I don't know this yet.

A transaction that assigns copyright ownership to a song cannot be directly stored in blocks on a blockchain. Blockchain technology is primarily designed to record and store transactions related to digital assets or data, such as cryptocurrencies or smart contracts. Issuing tokens for a carton of milk or issuing a degree can be represented as transactions on a blockchain through tokenization or credential verification mechanisms. However, assigning copyright ownership to a song involves legal complexities and requires external systems to enforce intellectual property rights, which cannot directly be stored on a blockchain.

What is a core domain for Accenture’s Multi-party Systems practice?


digital identity


social networking


employee retention


marketing responsiveness


I don't know this yet.

A core domain for Accenture's Multi-party Systems practice is digital identity. Multi-party Systems focus on enabling secure, scalable, and efficient digital identity management using blockchain and other related technologies. This includes establishing decentralized and trusted identity solutions that enhance privacy, security, and interoperability across multiple organizations or entities.

How could the adoption of blockchain within a supply chain help the world become more sustainable and, in some cases, eco-friendly?


Consumers could still purchase items at a store even if they forgot their wallet and phone at home.


Consumers will be able to effectively track where the components of their product were sourced.


Consumers will be able to store their personal identifiable information for recurring purchases.


Consumers could easily return and get refunds for products that didn't meet their expectations.


I don't know this yet.

The option that describes how the adoption of blockchain within a supply chain could help the world become more sustainable and eco-friendly is: "Consumers will be able to effectively track where the components of their product were sourced."

By implementing blockchain technology in supply chain management, the entire journey of a product can be recorded and made transparent. This allows consumers to trace the origin of components or raw materials used in a product, ensuring ethical sourcing practices and promoting sustainability. It enables consumers to make more informed choices and support companies that prioritize environmentally friendly practices. Additionally, blockchain can also facilitate the verification of certifications, such as organic or fair trade, further promoting sustainable and eco-friendly products.

What is a core domain for Accenture’s Multi-party Systems practice?


employee retention


marketing responsiveness


digital identity


social networking


I don't know this yet.

A core domain for Accenture's Multi-party Systems practice is digital identity. Multi-party Systems focuses on implementing and enabling secure, scalable, and efficient digital identity management solutions using technologies like blockchain. This includes establishing decentralized and trusted identity systems that enhance privacy, security, and interoperability across multiple entities or organizations.

How does blockchain technology help organizations when sharing data?


Data can be processed or viewed at only one node in the chain at any given time.


Data can only be exchanged through a central, trusted organization.


Everyone has transparency over how the data has been added.


Data can be edited or removed only through certain authorized central nodes.


I don't know this yet.

Blockchain technology helps organizations when sharing data through the principle of transparency and immutability. Everyone involved in the blockchain network has transparency over how the data has been added to the blockchain. Once data is added to a block and the block is added to the chain, it becomes extremely difficult to modify or remove that data. This ensures that the data shared among organizations remains immutable and tamper-proof.

Therefore, the closest option that aligns with the capabilities of blockchain technology is "Everyone has transparency over how the data has been added."

Blockchain maintains and records data and transactions in a new type of database system. This technology is used across organizations in an ecosystem and allows products’ histories to be captured as they move to the consumer.

How might this technology help inter-organization processes?


by limiting data sharing only to a single party


by creating an agile and dynamic database


by increasing mediators between transaction partners


by decreasing the likelihood of record tampering


I don't know this yet.

The technology of blockchain can help inter-organization processes by decreasing the likelihood of record tampering. As blockchain maintains an immutable and transparent ledger, the data and transactions recorded on the blockchain are resistant to tampering or unauthorized changes. This increases the integrity and trustworthiness of the records shared among organizations in an ecosystem. It reduces the risk of fraud or manipulation of data, enhancing the overall reliability and security of inter-organization processes.

Blockchain maintains and records data and transactions in a new type of database system. This technology is used across organizations in an ecosystem and allows products’ histories to be captured as they move to the consumer.

How might this technology help inter-organization processes?


by limiting data sharing only to a single party


by creating an agile and dynamic database


by increasing mediators between transaction partners


by decreasing the likelihood of record tampering


I don't know this yet.

The technology of blockchain can help inter-organization processes by creating an agile and dynamic database. With blockchain, organizations within an ecosystem can share and update data in a decentralized manner, eliminating the need for a central authority. This enables real-time visibility, transparency, and synchronization of data across multiple organizations. It enhances the efficiency and agility of inter-organization processes by enabling seamless collaboration, reducing the dependencies on intermediaries, and facilitating faster decision-making based on shared, up-to-date information.

A reputable, world-renowned auction house uses blockchain to verify the authenticity of paintings prior to placing them up for sale. The records that are stored on the various distributed ledgers for each painting include the artist’s credentials, history of ownership, appraisal values, and previous purchase price. Paula would like to make an offer on a painting.

After reviewing the distributed ledgers for the painting, why can Paula purchase the painting with confidence?


the ledgers are centralized, editable, and visible


the ledgers are protected, separate, and conventional


the ledgers are secure, shared, and incorruptible


the ledgers are private, accurate, and complete


I don't know this yet.

After reviewing the distributed ledgers for the painting, Paula can purchase the painting with confidence because the ledgers are secure, shared, and incorruptible. 

Blockchain technology ensures the security and integrity of the data recorded on the distributed ledgers. The ledgers are shared among multiple participants, making them transparent and accessible to relevant parties involved in the auction process. The distributed nature of the ledgers makes it difficult for anyone to tamper with or alter the information stored within them, increasing the trustworthiness and authenticity of the records. This gives Paula assurance that the painting's authenticity, artist's credentials, ownership history, and appraisal values are verifiable and reliable, providing her with confidence in her purchase decision.

A reputable, world-renowned auction house uses blockchain to verify the authenticity of paintings prior to placing them up for sale. The records that are stored on the various distributed ledgers for each painting include the artist’s credentials, history of ownership, appraisal values, and previous purchase price. Paula would like to make an offer on a painting.

After reviewing the distributed ledgers for the painting, why can Paula purchase the painting with confidence?


the ledgers are centralized, editable, and visible


the ledgers are protected, separate, and conventional


the ledgers are secure, shared, and incorruptible


the ledgers are private, accurate, and complete


I don't know this yet.

After reviewing the distributed ledgers for the painting, Paula can purchase the painting with confidence because the ledgers are secure, shared, and incorruptible.

The use of blockchain technology ensures that the ledgers storing the painting's information are secure, meaning they are protected against unauthorized access or tampering. The ledgers are also shared among multiple participants involved in the auction process, providing transparency and allowing relevant parties to access the information.

Moreover, blockchain's inherent properties make the ledgers incorruptible, making it extremely difficult to alter or manipulate the data stored on them. This ensures the integrity and authenticity of the records, including the artist's credentials, ownership history, appraisal values, and previous purchase price.

Therefore, Paula can rely on the verified and immutable information stored on the blockchain's distributed ledgers, giving her confidence in her purchase decision.

What is true about hashing?


Hashing is repeatable and is dependent upon the original data.


Hashing is equivalent to encryption.


Hashing is when the original data gets larger and larger.


Hashing is reversible.


I don't know this yet.

Hashing is repeatable and is dependent upon the original data. When data is processed through a hash function, it generates a fixed-size output, known as a hash code or hash value. The same input data will always produce the same hash value, ensuring repeatability. Additionally, the hash value is based on the input data, so even a small change in the input will result in a different hash value. This property makes hashing useful for data integrity verification and storing passwords securely.

It is important to note that hashing is different from encryption. Hashing is a one-way process, meaning it is not reversible. The original data cannot be obtained from the hash value. In encryption, the original data can be retrieved using a decryption process with the appropriate key.

What is true about hashing?


Hashing is repeatable and is dependent upon the original data.


Hashing is equivalent to encryption.


Hashing is when the original data gets larger and larger.


Hashing is reversible.


I don't know this yet.

What is true about hashing is that it is repeatable and dependent upon the original data. When the same data is processed through a hash function, it will always produce the same hash value. Additionally, even a small change in the input data will result in a significantly different hash value. This property ensures that the integrity of data can be verified by comparing hash values.

However, it is not true to say that hashing is equivalent to encryption. Hashing is a one-way process, meaning it is not reversible. The original data cannot be retrieved from the hash value alone. On the other hand, encryption involves converting data into a coded format that can be reversed using a decryption process with a specific key.

To summarize, hashing is repeatable and dependent upon the original data, but it is not reversible like encryption.

Accenture is a founding member of ID 2020.

What is the focus of this alliance?


blockchain


supply chain


digital identity


financial services


I don't know this yet.

ID2020 is an alliance that brings together various organizations, including Accenture, with the focus on digital identity. The alliance seeks to address the challenges of ensuring individuals' rights to have a legal and recognized digital identity, particularly for those who lack formal identification. The objective is to leverage technology, such as blockchain and other innovative solutions, to provide secure, portable, and user-controlled digital identities. The focus of ID2020 is not on blockchain itself but on addressing digital identity challenges globally.

A company wishes to begin the process of implementing a Multi-party System and is looking for other companies with similar interests to join them. With blockchain, every organization sees the same data.

What does this provide?

When implementing a Multi-party System with blockchain, every organization involved sees the same data, which provides a shared and synchronized view of the information. This shared data visibility allows for enhanced collaboration, transparency, and trust among the participating organizations. It eliminates the need for reconciliation of different datasets or informational disparities, as all parties have access to the same information in real-time. The shared view of data promotes accuracy, efficiency, and consensus among the organizations, enabling streamlined processes and improved decision-making.

by providing anonymity for each producer


by providing traceability of products


by ensuring known-traveler digital identity


by eliminating the need for smart contracts


I don't know this yet.

From the options provided, the statement that aligns with the concept of every organization seeing the same data in a Multi-party System with blockchain is "by providing traceability of products."

With blockchain, the shared and synchronized view of data allows for the traceability of products throughout the supply chain. Each organization involved can access the same information about the product's origin, manufacturing process, handling, and other relevant details. This transparency and traceability enable organizations to track and verify the authenticity, quality, and compliance of products at each stage of their journey. It can help mitigate issues such as counterfeiting, fraud, or unethical practices, ultimately enhancing accountability and trust among stakeholders.

As Accenture explores an end-to-end business flow that has reconciliation between multiple parties, what would we uncover?


trapped value


shared state


ownership


transparency


I don't know this yet.

As Accenture explores an end-to-end business flow that has reconciliation between multiple parties, we would uncover shared state.

Shared state refers to the synchronized and consistent view of data and the current state of a system among multiple parties involved in a business flow. In a blockchain-based multi-party system, all participants share the same data, ensuring that they have a common understanding of the current state of the shared system. This shared state eliminates the need for extensive reconciliation processes and reduces potential discrepancies or conflicts among the participating parties. It promotes transparency, efficiency, and collaboration by ensuring that everyone has access to the same information and can make decisions based on a shared understanding of the system's state.

Which statement is true about blockchain?


Blockchain encourages trust among all peers.


Blockchain guarantees the accuracy of the data.


Blockchain always requires a central authority as an intermediary.


Blockchain enables users to verify that data tampering has not occurred.


I don't know this yet.

The true statement about blockchain is that it enables users to verify that data tampering has not occurred.

Blockchain technology uses cryptographic techniques and a decentralized network of participants to ensure the integrity and immutability of data stored on the blockchain. By design, blockchain provides transparency and allows users to validate and verify the authenticity of data without relying on a central authority. The decentralized nature of blockchain ensures that any changes or tampering attempts to the data can be easily detected, providing assurance and trust in the accuracy and reliability of the information stored on the blockchain.

A software giant is enabling a city with smart parking, using real-time information captured by sensors on the vehicle and sensors in parking lots. When the vehicle leaves, data can be shared between the vehicle and parking system, which will prompt a payment from the driver’s payment device stored in their account.

How would the use of blockchain to store this data help reduce potential risks?


by sharing data with law enforcement authorities when requested


by easily monitoring traffic congestion in areas of the city


by creating a verified data record to a car’s digital identity


by storing large amounts of data without any delays


I don't know this yet.

The use of blockchain to store the data in this scenario would help reduce potential risks by creating a verified data record to a car's digital identity.

Blockchain technology allows for the creation of immutable and tamper-proof records. In the context of smart parking, blockchain can be used to create a verified data record for each vehicle's digital identity. This record can include information such as ownership history, payment details, and other relevant data.

By storing this information on a blockchain, it ensures that the data cannot be easily altered or manipulated. This reduces risks associated with fraudulent activities, unauthorized access to data, or disputes over payment transactions. It creates a trustworthy and reliable source of information that can be accessed by the relevant parties, such as parking system operators, drivers, and payment providers.

Therefore, using blockchain to store this data helps mitigate potential risks by providing a secure and verified data record that adds authenticity and integrity to the smart parking system.

An Accenture client is eager to begin their Cloud transformation but needs guidance on selecting the right approach to meet their specific needs and objectives.

Which tool can help advise this client on the right Cloud solution?


Cloud Portal


myWizard


myNav


Amazon AWS


I don't know this yet.

The tool that can help advise the client on the right Cloud solution is myNav.

myNav is an Accenture tool designed to assist clients in determining the most suitable Cloud strategy and solution based on their specific needs, objectives, and business requirements. It provides guidance and recommendations by considering various factors such as workload characteristics, technical feasibility, cost considerations, and regulatory requirements. myNav aims to optimize the Cloud transformation journey and help clients make informed decisions about their Cloud strategy and implementation plans.

What is Accenture doing to demonstrate our commitment to the Cloud space?


splitting our Cloud practice into separate, independent groups


helping our clients maintain their locally-hosted, legacy systems


shifting the responsibility for building Cloud solutions to our clients


making a multibillion-dollar investment in our Cloud capabilities


I don't know this yet.

Accenture is demonstrating its commitment to the Cloud space by making a multibillion-dollar investment in its Cloud capabilities. This investment reflects Accenture's dedication to expanding and enhancing its expertise, services, and offerings in the Cloud domain.
